pattern: Use a monotonic stack to count, for each right boundary element, how many valid left boundaries exist with the same value and no larger element in between.
hints: Every single-element subarray qualifies automatically, so start with n.
For a subarray [l..r], both nums[l] and nums[r] must equal the subarray maximum; this means no element strictly between l and r can exceed nums[l] = nums[r].

Maintain a decreasing stack of (value, count) pairs. When you encounter nums[i], pop all smaller values, then the count of equal values at the top is the number of valid left endpoints you can pair with i as the right endpoint.

edge_inputs.description: two equal maximums with smaller elements in between — they cannot pair
large peak separates equal boundary values

stress.description: random array with small value range to force many equalities
